penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Sixth video (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29, V30) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. GPT-4o's rubric-strict 2.5 is the correct rubric application. The pattern is now persistent across both equipment-disaster and recovery videos, confirming it is a prompt-level scoring drift rather than a recording-quality artifact.

phase_attribution_disagreement.claude_diagnosis: Phase split: load 3s, placement 25s, throws 62s (30+14+18), cut 70s. Cutting is the dominant loss center at 44% of total time.
phase_attribution_disagreement.gpt4o_diagnosis: Phase split: needle_load 5.8s, placement 43.1s, first 34.9s, second 25.3s, third 29.7s, cut 22.7s. Placement and throws are the dominant loss centers; cutting is fast.
phase_attribution_disagreement.analysis: INVERSE phase-split disagreement relative to V28. Same convergent-tails pattern (endpoint delta 3.8s) but Claude bundles ~50s of work into the cut phase that GPT-4o keeps in placement and throws. Both diagnoses agree the run is efficiency-limited, not technique-limited; they disagree on which window the inefficiency lives in. Worth flagging as the V30 mirror of the V28 boundary issue: cut phase definition needs to be tied to scissors entry, not visual knot completion.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Tightest score agreement in the series at delta 1.3 FLS. Reconciled value is 435.7 (GPT-4o, rubric-strict). The 1.3-point delta is the net of Claude's 2.5-point out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty offsetting Claude's 3.8-second time advantage. Both teachers independently identify V30 as a recovery run on a third equipment configuration, validating the equipment-specific framing of the V28-V29 catastrophe.

systematic_findings.tightest_score_agreement_in_series: FLS delta 1.3 is the smallest non-zero score gap recorded (V28 hit exact 0.0 by coincidence). Direct-camera recordings continue to compress the teacher-disagreement band: V30 confidence 0.62/0.74 — both at series highs.
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_now_persistent: Sixth out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty case from Claude (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29, V30). Pattern appears regardless of recording quality or run difficulty. Recommend formalizing the rubric-allowed penalty set in the next prompt iteration and adding a separate cosmetic_quality field to capture Claude's signal without distorting the FLS score.
systematic_findings.inverse_phase_boundary_disagreement: V28 and V30 form a mirror pair on the cut-phase boundary: V28 has Claude bundling work INTO cut (135s vs GPT 15s), V30 has GPT bundling work OUT OF cut (22.7s vs Claude 70s). Both cases reflect the same root-cause ambiguity about when 'tying' becomes 'trimming.' Suggested fix: define cut phase start as first scissors-in-field event, not knot-visually-seated.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_continues: V30 is another drain-closure agreement case. Running tally: AGREE 10 (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V26, V27, V28, V29, V30), DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).
systematic_findings.v28_through_v30_arc_validated_by_gpt4o: GPT-4o now independently corroborates the equipment-narrative across all three transition videos: V28 365/370s (catastrophe), V29 295/304.5s (adaptation), V30 158/161.8s (recovery on different new station). The blue-trainer recovery being on a SECOND new station rules out skill regression as the V28 explanation.
systematic_findings.lead_hand_disagreement_throw_1: First documented lead-hand disagreement on throw 1 (Claude right, GPT-4o left). Hand alternation pattern is preserved by both readings (R-L-R vs L-R-L), so neither incurs a hand-switch penalty. Worth flagging as a low-confidence attribute when the direct camera does not show wrist orientation clearly.
